question.text: What is the minimum investment amount required to open a Portfolio Management Services (PMS) account in India?

explanation.text: **The Logic:** SEBI mandates a minimum investment of Rs. 50 lakhs for PMS. This threshold ensures that only investors with sufficient net worth and presumably higher financial sophistication access this product, as PMS involves individual demat holdings with concentrated portfolios. **The Trap:** Rs. 1 crore is the minimum for AIF (Alternative Investment Fund), not PMS. Candidates frequently confuse these two HNI products. Rs. 25 lakhs was the older PMS threshold before SEBI revised it upward. **Cross-Exam Context:** NISM Series X-A covers PMS basics; NISM Series XII (Securities Markets Foundation) tests minimum investment thresholds across products. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** When comparing PMS (Rs. 50 lakhs) with AIF (Rs. 1 crore) and equity MF (Rs. 1,000), frame the conversation around the client's total investable surplus -- PMS makes sense only when the client has enough to diversify beyond just one PMS portfolio.
